name: Fly.io
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Fly.io's design language — an edge-compute platform with a deeply contrarian brand voice (engineering blog as marketing, irreverent copy, dense long-form posts) wrapped in a hot-pink-to-violet gradient identity, monospace-rich chrome, and a near-black canvas. The brand reads like an old-internet engineering newsletter that happens to sell global infrastructure.

design_language.motion_signature: Almost no motion — 150ms ease-out hover; the only signature animation is the hot-pink-to-violet gradient slow-drift on the brand-mark fly balloon, ~8s cycle.

trademark_signals: Hot-pink-to-violet gradient (#ff007a → #8957ff) as the brand's voltage moment — appears on the fly-balloon mark, on selected display words, and on the rare full-bleed CTA card
Long-form serif body — Lora (or PT Serif) for body text — refuses the SaaS sans-everywhere pattern; the brand wants to feel like reading a blog post
Code-block-as-content — homepage and product pages embed 6+ real shell snippets ('fly launch', 'fly deploy', 'fly logs') in JetBrains Mono with a near-black background
Irreverent technical copy — headlines like 'Speedrun an app in 5 minutes' or 'We're not a serverless company; we're a public utility' — the voice IS the brand
Near-black canvas (#0a0a0f) with subtle warm tint — refuses pure black and refuses navy
ai_slop_avoided: Don't drop the serif body face. Lora / PT Serif for body IS the brand's anti-SaaS signature
Don't soften the irreverent voice. The brand actively avoids 'enterprise-grade', 'leverage', 'unlock'
Don't use a static hero illustration. The brand uses real shell snippets as visual proof
Don't substitute the pink-violet gradient with magenta-only or violet-only — both colors must be present in the gradient
Don't add corporate trust signals (Fortune 500 logos, compliance badges) above the fold. The brand earns trust through technical proof, not logos
